teaching_language: English and Italian (the English-taught curricula include Accounting, Control and Corporate Finance, and Banking and Finance).

academic_requirements.summary: Applicants need a bachelor's degree or equivalent and must show a strong academic background in Accounting and Finance, together with supporting preparation in Management and in Economics and Quantitative subjects. Country-based entry title and GPA requirements also apply.
academic_requirements.prior_degree_or_diploma: Bachelor diploma or equivalent; a minimum three-year undergraduate degree (or equivalent) is required in Accounting and Finance or related fields.
academic_requirements.subject_requirements: Applicants must prove extensive academic skills in Accounting and Finance, plus proven academic skills in Management and in Economics and Quantitative subjects.
academic_requirements.gpa_or_country_requirements: Country-based specific entry title and GPA requirements apply. GMAT or GRE and a C-level English certificate are preferential, but not mandatory, and are considered only if completed before the application date.

language_requirements.required_level: B2 English (CEFR). A C-level English certificate is a preferential element for admission.
accepted_proofs: A recognised English certificate meeting the University minimum scores, such as TOEFL iBT 80, IELTS 6.0, Cambridge B2 First 173, or PTE Academic 65.
A previous qualification completed entirely in English, documented through self-certification or an official statement showing the language of instruction.
A provisional statement of results from the certifying body is accepted while waiting for the final certificate.
exemption_rules: Applicants whose bachelor's degree or equivalent entry qualification was completed entirely in English are exempt from submitting a separate English certificate.
Native English speakers from recognised countries, including the United States, are exempt from submitting a separate English certificate.
language_requirements.deadline: [uncertain] The common University language-requirement page states that proof of English for open-access degree programmes must be uploaded by 31 August 2026, but the programme-specific apply page does not restate a separate deadline.

application_deadline_eu.notes: Calls Two and Three are restricted to EU and EU-equated applicants.

application_deadline_non_eu.call_two: Not applicable for non-EU applicants residing outside Italy.
application_deadline_non_eu.call_three: Not applicable for non-EU applicants residing outside Italy.
application_deadline_non_eu.notes: The official apply page restricts Calls Two and Three to EU and EU-equated applicants.

required_documents.notes: Upload a colour scanned PDF of the academic transcript.

required_documents.document_name: Bachelor's degree or provisional academic qualification certificate
required_documents.notes: If the final qualification has not yet been awarded, an enrolment or provisional certificate is accepted.
EU citizens and non-EU citizens residing in Italy may use an ID card; other applicants use a passport.
Required only for non-EU citizens residing in Italy.

required_documents.document_name: English language certificate or other accepted proof
required_documents.notes: Required unless the applicant qualifies for an official exemption.

required_documents.document_name: Official English or Italian translations of academic documents
required_documents.notes: Required if the original documents are issued in another language.

required_documents.document_name: Original and translated/legalised copies of previous qualifications
required_documents.notes: If admitted, applicants must later submit original and translated/legalised qualification documents. No legalised documents are required at application stage.

entry_exam_or_test.test_name: No standard entry test; possible online interview; optional GMAT or GRE as preferential evidence
entry_exam_or_test.applies_to: The Commission may invite selected applicants to an online interview; GMAT or GRE may be submitted by any applicant as a preferential element.
entry_exam_or_test.dates: No fixed interview schedule is published; interviews are arranged only if requested by the Commission.
entry_exam_or_test.scoring_or_threshold: No mandatory test score threshold is published. GMAT or GRE scores and a C-level English certificate are preferential, but not mandatory.

source_quotes.quote: Open access with assessment test and admission requirements
Whenever necessary, the Commission may also ask candidates to sit an on-line interview.

uncertainty_notes: No standalone 2026/27 programme-specific admission PDF for international applicants was found; the official apply.unipd.it course page and common University admissions pages were used.
The official programme page is bilingual; this JSON summarizes the English-taught admission route described on the official apply page.
The University website states that only the official Italian version has legal validity; this JSON is based on the English official pages requested for the task.
The programme-specific apply page states the B2 English requirement but does not restate a separate proof-upload deadline, so the common University language page was used for that deadline rule.
